name: CuPy (NumPy on CUDA GPU)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Preferred Networks; NVIDIA GPU required

auth.notes: Local GPU library; NVIDIA CUDA required

performance.notes: 10-100x faster than NumPy for large array operations on GPU; kernel fusion

agent_readiness.notes: NumPy-compatible array library running on NVIDIA GPU via CUDA. Drop-in replacement: `import cupy as np`. Same API as NumPy — minimal code changes to move computation to GPU. Linear algebra, FFT, random number generation, sparse matrices. Custom CUDA kernel support (raw kernels, elementwise, reduction). cuSPARSE, cuBLAS, cuFFT under the hood. Part of RAPIDS ecosystem. Essential for GPU-accelerated numerical computing without writing CUDA.

gotchas: NVIDIA GPU required (no AMD/Intel GPU support)
GPU memory management (must explicitly free memory)
Large numerical workloads only — small arrays have overhead
best_when: GPU-accelerating existing NumPy code with minimal changes
Large matrix operations and FFT on GPU
RAPIDS data science pipeline GPU acceleration
Scientific computing needing GPU without CUDA C
avoid_when: When JAX/PyTorch provide better ML-specific GPU operations
summary: CuPy is a NumPy-compatible array library for NVIDIA GPU computation. Drop-in replacement (import cupy as np) that runs NumPy operations on GPU. 10-100x faster for large arrays. Part of RAPIDS ecosystem. Custom CUDA kernel support. Best for GPU-accelerating existing NumPy scientific computing code with minimal code changes. Requires NVIDIA GPU and CUDA installation.
